intro: Geissanthus lepidotus is a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Geissanthus lepidotus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
